issues.detail: Prop 'qa' (string, from QAProps) is missing from JSON. TSX destructures it and applies as data-qa attribute.

JSON props array is entirely empty. TSX Props type defines: children (React.ReactNode), labelClassName (string), title (string), disabled (boolean, default false), size ('s'|'m'|'l'|'xl', default 'm'), control (React.ReactElement, required), className (string), style (React.CSSProperties), qa (string). All are missing.

JSON types 'children' as '(status: "pending" | "success" | "error") => React.ReactElement' (function-only). TSX allows 'React.ReactElement | CopyToClipboardContent' — a plain React element is also valid.
JSON lists 'style' (React.CSSProperties) but DefinitionListProps does not extend DOMProps and has no style property. It extends AriaLabelingProps and QAProps only.
Prop 'groupLabelClassName' (string, optional) is defined in DefinitionListProps in types.ts but absent from JSON.

JSON types 'onClose' as '() => void'. TSX signature is '(event: MouseEvent | KeyboardEvent, reason: ModalCloseReason | "closeButtonClick") => void'. The event and reason parameters are entirely absent from the JSON type.
JSON lists 'style' (React.CSSProperties) but DialogProps extends AriaLabelingProps and QAProps only — no DOMProps, so no 'style' property exists on the interface.

Many props present in DialogProps are absent from JSON: 'modalClassName' (string), 'initialFocus' (ModalProps['initialFocus'] | 'cancel' | 'apply'), 'returnFocus' (ModalProps['returnFocus']), 'disableHeightTransition' (boolean, default false), 'onOpenChange', 'onEscapeKeyDown', 'onEnterKeyDown', 'onOutsideClick', 'onTransitionIn', 'onTransitionInComplete', 'onTransitionOut', 'onTransitionOutComplete', 'container' (HTMLElement).
JSON lists 'style' (React.CSSProperties) but DisclosureProps extends QAProps only and has no 'style' property.
Prop 'children' (React.ReactNode, optional) is in DisclosureProps but absent from JSON.
JSON lists 'onResizeComplete' but TSX has no such prop. The TSX resize callbacks are 'onResizeStart', 'onResize', and 'onResizeEnd', all typed as OnResizeHandler = (size: number) => void.
Props 'onResize' and 'onResizeEnd' (both OnResizeHandler = (size: number) => void) are in DrawerProps but absent from JSON.
JSON types 'onResizeStart' as '() => void'. TSX OnResizeHandler is '(size: number) => void' — the size argument is missing from the JSON type.

Many inherited ModalProps are in DrawerProps but absent from JSON: 'children' (React.ReactNode), 'contentClassName' (string), 'disableEscapeKeyDown' (boolean), 'initialFocus', 'returnFocus', 'disableBodyScrollLock' (boolean, default false), 'disableVisuallyHiddenDismiss' (boolean), 'floatingRef', 'disablePortal' (boolean), 'keepMounted' (boolean, default false), 'container', 'onTransitionIn', 'onTransitionInComplete', 'onTransitionOut', 'onTransitionOutComplete'.
JSON lists 'style' (React.CSSProperties) but DropdownMenuProps does not extend DOMProps or any interface with a style property.

Several props in DropdownMenuProps are absent from JSON: 'switcher' (React.ReactNode, deprecated), 'switcherWrapperClassName' (string), 'defaultSwitcherProps' (ButtonProps), 'defaultSwitcherClassName' (string), 'onSwitcherClick' (React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLElement>), 'menuProps' (Partial<MenuProps>), 'popupProps' (Partial<PopupProps>).

JSON types 'onClick' as '(file: File) => void'. TSX has 'onClick?: React.MouseEventHandler<HTMLDivElement>' which is '(event: React.MouseEvent<HTMLDivElement>) => void' — receives a mouse event, not the File object.

Flex extends BoxProps which contributes 'as' (keyof JSX.IntrinsicElements), 'children' (React.ReactNode), 'className' (string), 'style' (React.CSSProperties), 'spacing' (SpacingProps), 'overflow' ('hidden'|'x'|'y'|'auto'), and standard HTML attributes. None of 'as', 'children', 'className', 'style', or 'spacing' appear in the JSON props array.

JSON lists 'buttonProps' (React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>) as a prop. TSX has no such prop — HelpMarkProps extends React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> directly, so button attributes are passed as individual top-level props via ...restProps, not via a buttonProps object.
JSON types 'popoverProps' as 'Partial<PopoverProps>'. TSX has 'Omit<PopoverProps, "children">' — the children property is explicitly excluded from the type.
JSON types 'size' as 'number'. TSX has 'size?: number | string' — string values are also valid.
JSON types 'color' as 'string'. TSX has 'color?: ColorTextBaseProps["color"]' which is a restricted union of design-system color tokens ('(typeof TEXT_COLORS)[number]'), not a plain CSS string.

description_pairs.data_description: > The `unstable_ColorPicker` component is an experimental color selection component. The component is unstable, > so it means breaking changes can occur during minor or patch releases. Be aware of that.

Center you content in horizontal direction. > In most cases must be one on the page. ```tsx import {Container, Row, Col} from '@gravity-ui/uikit'; <Container masWidth="m"> <Row> <Col> Col 1 </Col> <Col> Col 2 </Col> </Row> </Container> ``` --- Storybook - https://preview.gravity-ui.com/uikit/?path=/docs/layout--playground#container
A headless component that provides clipboard copy functionality via a render prop pattern. Manages copy status and exposes it to the children function.
Displays a structured list of term-definition pairs. Supports vertical and horizontal layouts, responsive mode, and optional copy-to-clipboard per item.
A modal dialog with a structured layout of header, body, and footer sections. Built on top of Modal with a close button and standard slot components.
A collapsible section with a summary trigger and expandable content area. Supports controlled and uncontrolled modes.
A thin horizontal or vertical line used to separate sections of content. Supports an optional label and alignment.
A sliding panel that appears from the edge of the screen. Supports left, right, top, and bottom placement, optional resizing, and backdrop dismissal.
A button that opens a dropdown menu with a list of actions. Supports nested submenus, icons, disabled items, and a custom switcher trigger.
Displays a preview tile for a file with its name, type icon or image thumbnail, and optional action buttons. Used in file upload UIs.

Flexbox model utility component. ```tsx import {Flex, Button} from '@gravity-ui/uikit'; <Flex space > <Button> Button 1 </Button> <Button> Button 2 </Button> </Flex> ``` Use build in media goods via props ```tsx <Flex space={{s: '1', m: '5'}} direction={{'s': 'column', 'm': 'row'}} > {...} </Flex> ``` --- Storybook - https://preview.gravity-ui.com/uikit/?path=/docs/layout--playground#flex
A small question mark button that shows a popover with help content when clicked or hovered. Used for inline contextual help.
Renders a keyboard shortcut visually as individual key badges. Handles platform-specific key symbols for Mac and PC.
Renders an SVG icon from the Gravity UI icon set. Accepts icon data and supports size, color, and fill/stroke overrides.
